source: Kenya National Clinical Guidelines (2022) + WHO Emergency Triage Protocols

red_flags.label: Chest pain or tightness
red_flags.rationale: May indicate acute myocardial infarction or pulmonary embolism

red_flags.label: Difficulty breathing
red_flags.rationale: Severe dyspnoea may indicate respiratory failure, anaphylaxis, or cardiac event

red_flags.label: Loss of consciousness
red_flags.rationale: Syncope or coma requires immediate evaluation

red_flags.label: Signs of stroke
red_flags.rationale: Stroke symptoms require thrombolysis within 4.5 hours (FAST protocol)

red_flags.label: Severe allergic reaction
red_flags.rationale: Anaphylaxis can cause airway obstruction within minutes

red_flags.label: Very high fever with stiff neck
red_flags.rationale: Signs of bacterial meningitis – a life-threatening emergency

red_flags.label: Seizure or convulsion
red_flags.rationale: Status epilepticus is a medical emergency

red_flags.label: Severe abdominal pain
red_flags.rationale: May indicate perforated ulcer, appendicitis, or ectopic pregnancy rupture

red_flags.label: Suicidal ideation
red_flags.rationale: Immediate mental health crisis intervention required

red_flags.label: High fever in infant
red_flags.rationale: Fever >38°C in infants under 3 months requires emergency assessment
